extraction.one_line_summary: PEDERSEN Mads won reduced uphill sprint after Trek-Segafredo leadout by STUYVEN, with fastest sprinters dropped before 20km by lumpy circuit pace.
tactical_patterns: DSM's EEKHOFF double-attacked gaining 30s, forcing teams to burn matches before circuit controlled chaotic intermediate.
Trek isolated PEDERSEN for uphill finish by having STUYVEN execute leadout, unusual reversed leadout priority for World Champion.
Bonus sprint inside 20km became selection mechanism: LAPORTE 3s, ROGLIC 2s, WELLENS 1s while pure sprinters already dropped.
Movistar attempted GARCIA CORTINA setup recognizing reduced field, showing teams pivot tactics when A-tier sprinters eliminated early.

rider_signals.EEKHOFF Cees: positive: twice attacked solo gaining significant gaps, DSM using him as aggressor rather than domestique role
rider_signals.PEDERSEN Mads: positive: first WT win in extended drought, climbed well enough on lumpy circuit to survive while faster sprinters dropped
rider_signals.STUYVEN Jasper: positive: executed leadout for PEDERSEN rather than sprinting himself, tactical maturity prioritizing team result
rider_signals.JAKOBSEN Fabio: negative: dropped before 20km mark on lumpy circuit, confirming climbing limitations in hilly sprint stages
rider_signals.BENNETT Sam: negative: eliminated on circuit alongside JAKOBSEN and GAVIRIA, same climbing weakness pattern
rider_signals.LAPORTE Christophe: neutral: kept yellow despite final km crash, took 3s bonus showing stage-racing awareness despite GC lead
